intro: Grammitis turrialbae, also known as Tico fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Grammitis turrialbae include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
